content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves sharply to the right ahead with the dashed center line bending rightward, and the car appears to be on the left side of the track entering this right-hand curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 28, target = (28 + 5) % 121 = 33
- Target waypoint 33: (7.2657, 1.8637)
- Car: x=6.9588, y=1.2047, heading=45.44°
- dy = 1.8637 - 1.2047 = 0.6590
- dx = 7.2657 - 6.9588 = 0.3069
- bearing = atan2(0.6590, 0.3069) = 65.05°
- delta = 65.05° - 45.44° = +19.61°
- |delta| ≈ 20° → moderate left, use 15° steering at speed 1.5

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves right ahead but waypoint 33 is still to the left of current heading (delta=+19.61°), so continuing with 15° positive steering to follow the waypoint line through the curve apex.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track has a long straight heading east (~0°), then sharp right curve around WP26-38",
    "After right curve, track heads west around y=2.7-4.5 then loops back south",
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "On straights with |delta|<5°, use steering_angle=0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Car is left of center (~0.148m) — acceptable entering right curve, will naturally move right",
    "Speed 1.5 is safe on moderate curves; reduce to 1.0 or 0.75 for sharp turns",
    "Right curve apex is around WP32-34 (x~7.28, y~1.7-1.86), then heading reverses",
    "After WP34, heading will start decreasing (turning right/west) — prepare for negative steering",
    "Waypoint target index = nearest_waypoints[1]+5 consistently used",
    "Delta has been consistently +17 to +20° through this curve — smooth progression"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [6.800, 6.827, 6.897, 6.940, 6.959],
    "y": [0.990, 1.022, 1.110, 1.165, 1.205],
    "yaw": [32.700, 35.012, 40.254, 43.100, 45.440]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP33 at (7.266, 1.864): dy=0.659, dx=0.307, bearing=65.05°, delta=+19.61° → moderate left, steer 15°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 15.0
}
```
